evidence: The statement asks for the denominator of n!/(a!b!) to contain only bounded primes; factorials are the objects the problem is about.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; factorials n!/(a!b!) are the objects of the statement

evidence: The docstring asks that the denominator 'contains only primes ≪_C 1', so the prime divisors of the denominator are the core condition.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; 'primes <<_C 1' condition is the core of the statement

evidence: The problem asks for infinitely many pairs n < m with binom(2n,n) and binom(2m,m) having the same prime factors, formalized via Nat.centralBinom.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; Nat.centralBinom is the formal object

evidence: The docstring asks whether the two central binomial coefficients 'have the same set of prime divisors'.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; equal prime-factor sets is the defining condition

evidence: The solved variant exhibits a pair (n,m) with m ≠ n+1 whose central binomial coefficients share prime factors (found by AlphaProof).
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok (research solved); same centralBinom set S

evidence: The problem asks for a graph of infinite chromatic number (G.chromaticNumber = ⊤) with every n-vertex subgraph near-bipartite.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; G.chromaticNumber = top is in the formal statement

evidence: The condition is that every finite subgraph on n vertices can be made bipartite by deleting at most f(n) edges.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; IsBipartite after edge deletion is the core condition

evidence: The sqrt variant again requires a graph of infinite chromatic number whose subgraphs are near-bipartite within sqrt(n) edge deletions.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; sqrt variant, chromaticNumber = top in statement

evidence: The variant asks that every subgraph on n vertices can be made bipartite by deleting at most sqrt(n) edges.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; sqrt variant, bipartite-distance condition central

evidence: The problem is the infinitary version of chromatic number: a graph with chromatic number an infinite cardinal must contain an equal-chromatic-number subgraph.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; chromaticCardinal is the subject of the problem

evidence: The required subgraph must contain no odd cycle of length ≤ r, formalized via NoShortOddCycle over G.Walk cycles.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; NoShortOddCycle over G.Walk cycles, load-bearing; QID = cycle (graph theory)

evidence: The statement concerns the sumsets A+A, A1+A1 and A2+A2 of a decomposition A = A1 ⊔ A2.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; A+A, A1+A1, A2+A2 are the objects

evidence: The hypothesis and conclusion are that the sumsets have positive density (HasPosDensity), the literal-density interpretation.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; HasPosDensity = literal natural-density condition

evidence: The lower-density variant asks whether A+A of positive lower density forces a decomposition with both sumsets of positive lower density.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; sumsets central to lower variant

evidence: The variant is phrased in terms of positive lower density (0 < lowerDensity) of the sumsets.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; lowerDensity is a natural-density variant (same enwiki article)

evidence: The upper-density variant, proved by the DeepMind prover agent, concerns the sumsets A+A, A1+A1 and A2+A2.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok (research solved); sumsets central

evidence: The variant is phrased in terms of positive upper density (0 < upperDensity) of the sumsets.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; upperDensity is a natural-density variant (same enwiki article)

evidence: Part (ii) asks for a basis A of order 2 (IsAddBasisOfOrder (A ∪ {0}) 2) with no decomposition into two syndetic-sumset parts.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; IsAddBasisOfOrder 2 is the hypothesis; label matches Wikidata exactly

evidence: The condition is that A1+A1 and A2+A2 cannot both have bounded gaps (IsSyndetic on the sumsets).
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; syndetic sumsets are the conclusion

evidence: The Murty-Simon conjecture concerns diameter-2-critical graphs: diameter 2 and deleting any edge increases the diameter.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; IsDiameter2Critical via G.diam is the subject

evidence: The docstring states equality is conjectured to hold for the complete balanced bipartite graph K_{⌈n/2⌉,⌊n/2⌋}.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; K_{n/2,n/2} is the conjectured extremal graph stated in the docstring

evidence: Plesnik's bound |E(G)| < 3n(n-1)/8 holds for any diameter-2-critical graph on n vertices.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl Erdos742.variants.plesnik_bound exists (research solved); diameter-2-critical hypothesis

evidence: Fan verified the Murty-Simon edge bound for diameter-2-critical graphs with n ≤ 24 or n = 26.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl Erdos742.variants.fan_bound exists (research solved); diameter-2-critical hypothesis

evidence: Furedi proved the Murty-Simon edge bound for all sufficiently large diameter-2-critical graphs.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl Erdos742.variants.furedi_bound exists (research solved); diameter-2-critical hypothesis

evidence: The problem asks for A ⊆ N whose sumset A+A has lower density at least 1-ε while the representation function 1_A∗1_A stays bounded.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; A+A sumset density is the statement

evidence: The condition is on the lower density of A+A being at least 1-ε (lowerDensity in the formal statement).
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; lowerDensity (A+A) >= 1-eps is the condition

evidence: The problem asks for a graph of chromatic number aleph_1 (G.chromaticCardinal = ℵ_1) on aleph_1 vertices.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; chromaticCardinal = aleph_1 is the statement

evidence: Every n-vertex subgraph must contain an independent set of size > n^(1-ε) (G.IsIndepSet in the formal statement).
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; G.IsIndepSet of size > n^(1-eps) is the conclusion

evidence: The problem asks for a graph G of infinite chromatic number (G.chromaticNumber = ⊤) with large independent sets in every subgraph.
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; chromaticNumber = top in the statement

evidence: Every subgraph on m vertices must contain an independent set of size at least m/2 - f(m) (G.IsIndepSet).
verify_note: QID live, label exact; decl+attr ok; independent set of size m/2 - f(m) is the conclusion
